task.objective: Add a dedicated "Performance Impact" section to the comparison results that highlights tests with significant duration changes (regressions and improvements). This surfaces performance insights that are currently buried in the test diff table.

context.project_type: E2E Test Dashboard - Compare Page Enhancement
context.background:
The compare API already returns performance data for each test: - durationDeltaMs: change in milliseconds - durationDeltaPercent: percentage change - durationCategory: "regression" | "improvement" | "stable" | null
The API also returns a performanceSummary in the response: - regressions: count of tests with significant slowdown - improvements: count of tests with significant speedup - stable: count of tests with stable duration - threshold_pct: the threshold used (default 20%)
Currently this data is available but not prominently displayed. Users need to see at a glance: "This branch made 3 tests slower, 5 tests faster"
context.audience: - Developers monitoring performance impact of changes - QA engineers reviewing branch quality
context.impact: MEDIUM - Surfaces valuable performance data that's currently hidden

existing_code.file: export interface TestComparisonItem { testSignature: string testName: string testFile: string baselineStatus: 'passed' | 'failed' | 'skipped' | null currentStatus: 'passed' | 'failed' | 'skipped' | null baselineDurationMs: number | null currentDurationMs: number | null durationDeltaMs: number | null durationDeltaPercent: number | null diffCategory: TestDiffCategory durationCategory: 'regression' | 'improvement' | 'stable' | null }

functional.requirement: Show count of regressions, improvements, stable tests
Color-code: red for regressions, green for improvements
Show top 5 worst regressions with duration delta
Show top 5 best improvements with duration delta
Collapsed by default if no significant changes
Add filter button to show only performance regressions

solution.approach: 1. Create new PerformanceInsightsCard component 2. Import TestComparisonItem from lib/types.ts 3. Memoize filtered and sorted tests 4. Filter tests by durationCategory to find regressions/improvements 5. Sort by durationDeltaPercent to show worst/best first 6. Add collapsible sections with ARIA attributes 7. Integrate into compare-client.tsx after ComparisonSummaryCards

steps.step: 2. Compare two executions that have performance differences
4. If regressions exist, verify section is auto-expanded
5. Verify regressions sorted by severity (worst first)
6. Verify duration values and percentages are formatted correctly
7. Verify color coding (red for regressions, green for improvements)
1. Compare two identical or very similar executions
2. Verify "No significant performance changes" message appears
1. Navigate to performance insights section with screen reader
2. Verify badge counts are announced with context
4. Verify test list items are navigable

always.rule: Limit to top 5 items to avoid overwhelming
Auto-expand when regressions exist (important to surface)

never.rule: Never redefine types that exist in lib/types.ts
Never show empty sections (hide if no data)

output_format.structure: 1. Create `components/dashboard/performance-insights-card.tsx` 2. Update `app/dashboard/compare/compare-client.tsx` to import and render
output_format.validation: After implementation: 1. Verify component renders without errors 2. Test with various comparison results 3. Verify collapsed/expanded states work 4. Test accessibility with screen reader
